task_name: N/A - Educational presentation on robotic pelvic surgery training model
summary: This video is an academic presentation about the development of a robotic pelvic surgery training model and hierarchical task analysis. It contains title slides, objectives, model anatomy demonstrations (pelvic model with hands-on anatomical palpation), robotic surgical instrument demonstrations (cautery, scissors, graspers on mesh/tissue models), and summary slides. The content is educational in nature with no FLS task performance being scored.
video_classification_rationale: The video consists of presentation slides (title, objectives, summary) interspersed with demonstration segments showing anatomical models and robotic surgical instruments on bench/lab models. This is clearly instructional/educational content designed to convey information about a training program, not a formal FLS task performance by a trainee or surgeon attempting to complete a timed, scored task. No timer is visible, no formal task sequence is being executed, and the context is clearly academic presentation.
reason_no_score: This is an instructional/educational video presenting information about a robotic pelvic surgery training model development. It does not contain a formal FLS task performance (peg transfer, pattern cut, endoloop, extracorporeal suture, or intracorporeal suture). The segments showing instruments and models are demonstrative/educational, not scored performance attempts.

demonstrated_techniques: Pelvic anatomy identification on physical model
verbal_cues: Development and Hierarchical Task Analysis of a Robotic Pelvic Surgery Training Model
Objectives include creation of pelvic simulation model and development of HTA for robotic sacrocolpopexy
Assessment of model's ability to provide adequate training platform
teaching_content.presentation_context: Academic research presentation on surgical training model development from Atrium Health Division of Female Pelvic Medicine and Reconstructive Surgery
